evals.prompt: I want to integrate the PayPal API into my project so users can make payments.
evals.expected_output: The agent detects the project language, checks for missing guidelines/skills, calls fetch_api with the detected language, identifies the PayPal key from the results, then calls ask for integration guidance. It does NOT guess at SDK usage from its own knowledge.

assertions.description: Agent calls fetch_api to discover available APIs
fetch_api is called with language: typescript, inferred from package.json with typescript dep
Agent calls ask to get integration guidance, not its own knowledge
Agent does not write PayPal SDK code from its own knowledge before calling fetch_api

evals.prompt: Integrate the Stripe payment API into this project.
evals.expected_output: The agent calls fetch_api to discover available APIs. When Stripe is not in the results, it informs the user that Stripe is not currently available in this plugin and stops — it does NOT proceed with its own knowledge of the Stripe SDK.

assertions.description: Agent calls fetch_api first
Agent tells the user the API is not available in context-matic when not found in fetch_api results
Agent attributes the limitation to the plugin/context-matic, not a general statement
Agent does NOT install or use the Stripe SDK from its own knowledge

evals.prompt: Integrate the PayPal API. I already have the guidelines and conventions set up.
evals.expected_output: The agent reads the workspace, finds that guideline files and the language-conventions skill already exist, skips calling add_guidelines and add_skills, and proceeds directly to fetch_api.

assertions.description: Agent does NOT call add_guidelines when all guideline files already exist
Agent does NOT call add_skills when typescript-conventions already exists
Agent calls fetch_api after confirming guidelines/skills are present

evals.prompt: Add PayPal payments to my C# project.
evals.expected_output: The agent detects C# as the project language from the .csproj file and uses 'csharp' as the language parameter in all MCP tool calls.

assertions.description: fetch_api is called with language: csharp
Agent does not pass language: typescript for a C# project

evals.prompt: Set up PayPal payments in my Python app.
evals.expected_output: The agent detects Python from requirements.txt and uses 'python' in all context-matic tool calls.

files.content:
from flask import Flask
app = Flask(__name__)

assertions.description: fetch_api is called with language: python

evals.prompt: How does PayPal authentication work? What models are involved in creating a payment order?
evals.expected_output: The agent breaks the complex question into smaller focused queries: one ask call for authentication, one for creating a payment order, and uses model_search for relevant models — rather than sending a single broad query.

assertions.description: Agent makes a focused ask query about authentication
Agent makes a separate focused ask query about creating a payment/order
Agent uses model_search to look up model definitions

evals.prompt: I just ran `npm install @paypal/paypal-server-sdk` and it installed successfully. What's next?
evals.expected_output: The agent calls update_activity with milestone: sdk_setup because the SDK package has been concretely installed, then proceeds to guide the next step (auth configuration).

assertions.description: Agent calls update_activity with milestone: sdk_setup because SDK install is confirmed

evals.prompt: I'm thinking about integrating PayPal. Can you look up how authentication works in the SDK?
evals.expected_output: The agent calls fetch_api and ask to look up authentication guidance, but does NOT call update_activity — the user is only asking questions, no code or infrastructure change has been made.

assertions.description: Agent calls fetch_api to discover the API
Agent calls ask for authentication guidance
Agent does NOT call update_activity — no milestone has been reached, only questions asked

evals.prompt: I've now added my PayPal CLIENT_ID and CLIENT_SECRET to my .env file and I'm reading them in my config.ts. What should I do next?
evals.expected_output: The agent calls update_activity with milestone: auth_configured because credentials are both written into the runtime environment (.env) AND referenced in actual code (config.ts).

files.content:
export const paypalClientId = process.env.PAYPAL_CLIENT_ID!;
export const paypalClientSecret = process.env.PAYPAL_CLIENT_SECRET!;

assertions.description: Agent calls update_activity with milestone: auth_configured

evals.prompt: My PayPal API call is returning a 401 Unauthorized error. Here's the error: `{ "error": "invalid_client", "error_description": "Client Authentication failed" }`
evals.expected_output: The agent calls update_activity with milestone: error_encountered because the developer has reported a failing API call with an error response.

assertions.description: Agent calls update_activity with milestone: error_encountered for the reported 401 error
Agent does NOT call update_activity with error_resolved — the error has not been fixed yet

evals.prompt: I fixed the credential issue and my PayPal order creation call is working now — I can see the order ID in the response.
evals.expected_output: The agent calls update_activity with both error_resolved (fix applied, API call confirmed working) and first_call_made (first API call executed successfully).

assertions.description: Agent calls update_activity with milestone: error_resolved because fix was applied and call confirmed working
Agent calls update_activity with milestone: first_call_made because first API call executed successfully

evals.prompt: I need to integrate PayPal. I don't have any guidelines or conventions set up yet.
evals.expected_output: The agent finds no guideline files or language-conventions skill in the workspace, calls add_guidelines to add the missing guideline files, and calls add_skills to add the language conventions skill before proceeding.

assertions.description: Agent calls add_guidelines because guideline files are missing
Agent calls add_skills because typescript-conventions is missing
Agent calls fetch_api after setting up guidelines and skills

evals.prompt: Look up the Order model in the PayPal SDK for me.
evals.expected_output: The agent uses model_search with the API key and a relevant query to look up the Order model definition. It does NOT call update_activity — a model lookup is not an integration milestone.

assertions.description: Agent calls model_search to look up the Order model
model_search query references Order
Agent does NOT call update_activity for a model lookup — not an integration milestone

evals.prompt: Look up the createOrder endpoint in the PayPal SDK.
evals.expected_output: The agent calls endpoint_search with the API key and 'createOrder' as the query. It does NOT call update_activity — an endpoint lookup is not an integration milestone.

assertions.description: Agent calls endpoint_search to look up the createOrder endpoint
endpoint_search query references createOrder
Agent does NOT call update_activity for an endpoint lookup

evals.prompt: Add PayPal to my Go service.
evals.expected_output: The agent detects Go from go.mod and uses 'go' as the language in all context-matic tool calls.

assertions.description: fetch_api is called with language: go

evals.prompt: I'm starting fresh integrating PayPal. Don't use your own knowledge — use the context-matic tools.
evals.expected_output: The agent follows the full workflow: detects language, checks guidelines/skills, calls fetch_api, extracts the PayPal key, and uses ask for integration guidance. It passes the key extracted from fetch_api results into subsequent ask, model_search, and endpoint_search calls — it does NOT use a hardcoded or guessed key.

assertions.description: fetch_api is called before ask, model_search, or endpoint_search
ask is called with a key parameter (derived from fetch_api results, not hardcoded)
ask is called with a language parameter
